rationale: The cited paper is referenced multiple times in the body and helps structure the rejoinder's argument, rather than appearing only as a background list item. Franses explicitly says his main concern about policy analysis is emphasized by Van Heerde, Dekimpe, and Putsis (2005). He also uses their shared Albert Heijn pricing example when discussing expectations and when the Lucas critique matters. So the citation plays a meaningful interpretive and framing role, even though it is not a direct method-transfer or extension.
relation_description: The rejoinder engages Van Heerde, Dekimpe, and Putsis (2005) as a substantive discussion partner on policy simulation, expectations, and the Lucas critique. It uses that paper to reinforce key claims and to ground a concrete illustrative case in the argument.
